data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification). Verified each codebase assertion by Read/Grep:

- VERIFIED: `_io/import_ndjson.py` carries inline `# noqa: BLE001` at lines 225, 237, 243, 258, 271, 276, 289 (read confirms 225/237/243/258/271/276/289).
- VERIFIED: stderr-diagnostic prints exist in `_store/push.py` (lines 128/141/165/181/190/195), `_store/sync.py` (77/127/144), and `reducer/_processors.py` (144, and 578 = `file=sys.stderr`).
- VERIFIED: `_json_or` swallow exists — function at `__init__.py:230`, `except Exception: return default` at line 235 (plan cites :228, which is the comment header — minor line drift).
- GAP (load-bearing config mismatch): The plan says it will bring broad-excepts in `reducer/` and `_io/` to the floor and "remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist." But `pyproject.toml` per-file-ignores (lines 208-212) show: `__init__.py` = [BLE001] only; `reducer/*` = [T201] only; `_io/*` = [T201] only; only `graph/*` and `_store/*` carry [BLE001, T201]. So `reducer/*` and `_io/*` are NOT in the BLE001 allowlist — the `_io/import_ndjson.py` BLE001 suppressions are INLINE per-line noqas, not package-level. The AC "these packages are removed from the allowlist" therefore needs to distinguish which rule is removed per package, and the `_io/` BLE re-validation closes inline noqas (not an allowlist entry).
